programmes.show_description: No Time For F*cking Chemistry: While taking menacing steps to secure Nellie's land, Strickland instils confidence in Leary's political abilities. (S3, ep 3 of 10)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Action-packed documentary following rapid response officers, the fastest cops in Britain, as they crack down on crime. Time is of the essence! (S3, ep 2)
Bourbon Blues: Officers pull over a suspended driver with a box of bourbons by his side, a late night takeaway run turns sour and a gymnastics coach stretches himself thin. (S12, ep 7)
Old But Gold: An elderly woman drives with a suspended license, a young family's day turns from bad to worse and a driver has a terrible burnout. (S12, ep 8)
Follow the work of the New Zealand Customs agents in their quest to prevent illegal items passing through the country's borders. (S10, ep 1)
Follow the work of the New Zealand Customs agents in their quest to prevent illegal items passing through the country's borders. (S10, ep 2)
Documentary following the men and women who help keep motorists safe on some of the most perilous highways and byways of New Zealand. (S8, ep 5)

Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
A runaway driver clocks huge speeds as he flees from police, a motorcyclist goes head first through a car window and a man suffers an asthma attack - or does he? (S16, ep 3)
A driver flees a late-night crash scene; a chainsaw-wielding biker attracts police attention; and one of the weirdest breakdowns you'll see. (S16, ep 4)
A street performer is caught in the middle of an escape act, while a cheeky bus passenger answers questions about his mischievous past. (S2, ep 2)
A distinctive bag has officers seeing double, and a photographer misses the big picture. Plus, a heavily-modified truck causes concern. (S2, ep 3)
Fly-on-the-wall documentary following the men and women who defend Australia from drug runners, smugglers and terrorists.  (S4, ep 15)

Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 9)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 10)
A Canadian is interviewed under oath after CBP discovers pictures of pot plants on his phone. Without paperwork, it could be curtains for a Venezuelan conductor. (S1, ep 21)
CBP questions a Canadian attempting to enter the US with three of his American family members. An officer has no time for a Swiss traveller's watch declaration. (S1, ep 22)
A Brazilian man hiding a large quantity of cocaine is busted at Auckland Airport. Plus, a woman with undeclared seeds loses her cool with Biosecurity. (S11, ep 7)
Immigration deal with a Chinese national using a fake passport, while custom discover a huge stash of meth hidden among household items. (S11, ep 8)

Sanctuary: Kira finds herself in a dilemma when a displaced alien race arrives on Deep Space Nine and claims Bajor as its people's legendary homeland.
Watergate: When the Russians discover a stargate, they turn to SG-1 for help. (S4, ep 7)
Q2: When Q's son becomes reckless and uncontrollable, Q places him in Captain Janeway's care for a week. Can she transform the teen? (S7, ep 19)
Too Short A Season: The Enterprise escorts a Federation admiral to negotiate the release of hostages. But things take a turn when the crew discovers the planet's governor is after revenge.
Three visitors from Malaysia raise alarms when it emerges that they've cancelled their accommodation just before their flight and their lies begin to unravel. (S15, ep 9)
When a Chinese bulk carrier docks in Newcastle it appears there's been a major security breach and what the Border Force officers find is shocking. (S15, ep 10)
The Murder in the Tower Block: When a body is found in a flat, a trail of forensic evidence allows detectives to distinguish between friend and foe. (S2, ep 10)
The Murder with No Body: The tragic case of 22-year-old Helen McCourt, who got off a bus one night in her hometown but never arrived at her nearby home. (S2, ep 11)
Unexpected: After sharing a friendly moment with the female engineers of an alien ship, Trip discovers that he's just had his first experience with alien sex and is now pregnant!
In Chinatown, No One Thinks About Forever: Shaken by the loss of Nellie's land, Ah Toy grapples with a hard choice. Mai Ling makes a deal with Eliza. (S3, ep 4 of 10)
Action-packed documentary following rapid response officers, the fastest cops in Britain, as they crack down on crime. Time is of the essence! (S3, ep 3)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Pokie Pass Out: A man has a welfare check in a busy car park, an evening drive becomes a sobering experience for one driver and a man struggles to correctly do a drugs test. (S12, ep 9)
Ice Man Cometh: Bystanders call out police to deal with an erratic driver, an unlicensed driver tries to push his luck and a man is fined on the way home from the store. (S12, ep 10)

A returning American is cuffed at the Peace Arch crossing in Washington and in Detroit, a German rock band rolls straight into secondary. (S1, ep 13)
At Detroit Metro Airport, a traveller minding manners may need to mind his story instead and the Chicago canine unit knows smugglers don't play games. (S1, ep 14)
A Canadian crosses the Ambassador Bridge in Detroit by mistake and her bag of medicinal marijuana may cost her. (S1, ep 15)
In New York City, a laptop belonging to a Caribbean traveller does not compute and a young Brit arrives at Detroit Metro Airport to visit an American cyber pal. (S1, ep 16)
Documentary series following Australian Customs and Border Protection officers as they attempt to enforce the country's customs, quarantine and immigration laws. (S14, ep 3)
Documentary series following Australian Customs and Border Protection officers as they attempt to enforce the country's customs, quarantine and immigration laws. (S14, ep 4)
Trouble looms for a young Asian woman carrying someone else's bag, and a pair of Canadians return from a concert in California with more than a T-shirt. (S1, ep 13)

From some of the most heart stopping police chases ever seen, to smashing total wipeouts, we've got it all caught on the handy dashboard camera. (S3, ep 9)
Our series finale brings you the greatest smashes, extreme bouts of road rage and even the most bizarre traffic entertainment ever caught on camera. (S3, ep 10)
A look at the clever new methods the police and public are using to catch criminals. (S7, ep 2)

As the Zimbabwe and Zambia gangs negotiate predators as they forage on the river banks, the Victoria gang try to hide a new food source from baboons. (S1, ep 4)
The Kalahari: The immense desert of the Kalahari dominates southwestern Africa. Rain disappears as it quickly as falls, providing its plucky inhabitants with brutal conditions. (S1, ep 5)
Get to know the Waterfront Pride: Males Tom Tom and Caller, eight lionesses, 10 youths and Einstein, the grown cub who never left home. (2 of 5)
Find more spectacular crashes and roadside disasters in a new episode of Caught on Dashcam! (S6, ep 7)
Tense action thriller following government agent Liam Neeson who specialises in removing operatives whose covers have been exposed. (2022)
From epic road rage to instant karma, the consequences of a mid-life crisis, treacherous weather and construction chaos - we've got it all Caught on Dashcam! (S5, ep 4)
